sections.paragraph:
- Original: Ἰωράμ

- Transliteration: Ioram

- Phonetic: ee-o-ram'

- Definition: Jehoram = "whom Jehovah has exalted"

1. the son of Jehoshaphat, king of Judah, succeeded his father on the throne of Judah

- Origin: of hebrew origin H3141

- TDNT entry: None

- Part(s) of speech: Noun Masculine

- Strong's: Of hebrew origin [H3141]; Joram an Israelite: - Joram.
